free_text: Neutron capture yields and resonance parameters of
dysprosium isotopes measured at Japan Proton
Instrument at Materials and Life Science Experimental
Facility of Japan Proton Accelerator Research Complex
operated in single-bunch mode with a repetition rate
Proton beam: 3 GeV; single-bunch mode, pulse width of
100 ns, repetition rate of 25 Hz; 58 of 62 pulses
Boron carbide (B4C) and gold samples were used to
determine the incident neutron spectrum at the sample
position and the resolution function, respectively.
A Gaussian plus exponential function. The parameters
of the analytical form defined in the SAMMY code are:
- the uncertainties in the flight path length deltatL
- the full width at half maximum (FWHM) of the Gaussian
- the TOF channel width parameter deltatC
- the energy folding width of the exponential part
The energy-dependent deltatL and deltatE determined by
fitting of the 197Au yield data with the SAMMY code
The spectrometer consists of two NaI(Tl)
scintillators surrounded by an annular-shaped plastic
with diameters and lengths of 330 and 203, and 203 and
203 mm, were placed at detection angles of 90 degree
and 125 degree with respect to the neutron beam line,
respectively. The detector at 90 degree was used.
The following normalization factors were determined
